summary: Phase 9.A.1 (documentation polish + accessibility-CSS gate) approved. Phase 9 (per docs/PHASES.md:571-593) is the FINAL phase before the v1 release and includes 8 deliverables across documentation, UI, art tuning, audio, and accessibility. Several Phase 9 deliverables require either external input (audio assets, itch.io account, custom domain DNS, license-choice authorization) or significant subjective human judgment (atlas-recipe playtesting). The remainder are sandbox-resolvable polish tasks. Phase 9.A.1 in this iteration tackles the smallest tractable chunk: documentation-only updates + a low-risk accessibility CSS gate. The full Phase 9 → 9.A.* sub-phase split is recorded in next_phase below; the established X.A.N internal-split precedent from Phases 7 + 8 carries forward. Concrete deliverables: (1) README.md rewritten from a 38-line outdated stub (claimed 'Phase 1: in progress' which is misleading post-Phase-8) to a 178-line production-ready document covering: updated project status (Phase 8 complete; Phase 9 polish in progress); user-facing 'how to share a run' walk-through (mint via diagnostic Share UI → recipient redirected via auto-redirect path); user-facing 'how to verify a shared run' walk-through (paste seed/fp/hash/log → click Verify → JSON discriminated-union result); daily-seed convention (`?seed=YYYY-MM-DD` per docs/PHASES.md:585 deliverable); local-development quick-start (npm scripts); deploy structure (canonical URLs for latest/ + releases/<commit>/ + releases/index.json); architecture overview (8-layer table mirroring docs/ARCHITECTURE.md); performance notes (41 KB gzipped JS bundle under the 110 KB CI gate; 1610-byte atlas binary; cross-OS atlas-equality CI matrix); browser support (latest two stable Chromium/Firefox/WebKit); accessibility notes (keyboard-only nav, prefers-reduced-motion respected, focus-visible outline); cross-runtime determinism golden chain table (RANDOM_WALK + MAPGEN + SIM + ATLAS + 4 preset-seeds + INVENTORY + WIN + REPLAY); license placeholder ('A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish' — deferred because license choice requires explicit maintainer authorization and was not provided in this iteration). (2) style.css gains 2 new accessibility blocks at the tail (lines 316-348): @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override that caps animation-duration / animation-iteration-count / transition-duration / scroll-behavior — preventive gate since the page currently has no animations on the state-hash chain but future polish iterations may add UI animations; :focus-visible outline using the existing --accent neon-purple color for high-contrast keyboard-focus indication. Both blocks are pure CSS (no JS state); no runtime impact on the deterministic core. All sandbox gates green: 1154 tests passing across 81 test files (unchanged from Phase 8.B — Phase 9.A.1 is documentation + CSS only); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.css = 4.26 KB raw / 1.36 KB gzipped (was 3.99 / 1.24 in Phase 8.B = +0.27 KB raw / +0.12 KB gzipped from the 2 new CSS blocks); JS bundle dist/assets/index-*.js = 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped (unchanged). Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). Phase 9.A.2+ deliverables (title screen, theme registry, CRT shader, LICENSE creation, CONTRIBUTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md polish, formal accessibility audit, Lighthouse > 90 verification on live deploy) are deferred to subsequent iterations; the audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain blocked on external input.
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.1: README rewrite (Phase 8 complete + share/verify walk-throughs + daily-seed convention + architecture overview + perf + accessibility notes); style.css prefers-reduced-motion + :focus-visible accessibility gates

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.1's documentation polish + accessibility CSS gate (a) corrects the stale 'Phase 1 in progress' README banner so first-time visitors aren't misled about the project's current state, (b) documents the Phase 8 share/verify workflows for users who want to mint or verify a shared URL without reading the full memo, (c) documents the daily-seed convention per docs/PHASES.md:585 deliverable, (d) lands a preventive prefers-reduced-motion gate so future Phase 9 UI animations honor the user's system preference, and (e) preserves every Phase 8 + prior invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; existing test goldens preserved; all gates green). Phase 9 is the FINAL phase per docs/PHASES.md:571-593; Phase 9.A.1 is the smallest tractable sub-chunk that an unattended-mode iteration can complete without requesting external input (audio assets, license-choice authorization, custom domain DNS) or human aesthetic judgment (atlas-recipe playtesting).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1154 passed across 81 test files (no net change vs Phase 8.B — Phase 9.A.1 is documentation + CSS only, no production-code or test changes). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. CSS files are not lint-scoped (eslint targets .ts only); the README.md is not lint-scoped either. The Phase 8.A.3 dist-build-* + dist-final ignore extension continues to hold.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. No TypeScript changes in 9.A.1.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.css = 4.26 KB raw / 1.36 KB gzipped (was 3.99 / 1.24 in 8.B = +0.27 KB raw / +0.12 KB gzipped from the 2 new accessibility CSS blocks). dist/assets/index-*.js = 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped (unchanged from 8.B — no JS or TS changes). Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible behavioral changes in 9.A.1. The diagnostic page + playable game + auto-redirect + Share This Run + Verify Pasted Log + Save Slots + Replay This Run sections from Phase 8 are unchanged. The only user-visible additions are: (a) keyboard-focus rings now use the high-contrast --accent (neon-purple) color, replacing the browser default outline; (b) on systems where the user has set `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, all CSS transitions / animations are capped at 0.001ms (preventive — the page currently has no animations to throttle; the gate is for future polish iterations). Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner); the new CSS additions are visually verifiable on the live deploy.

readme_polished.evidence: README.md rewritten from 38 lines (outdated 'Phase 1: in progress' banner + bare-bones quick-start) to 178 lines covering: project status reflecting Phase 8 complete; live-URL + content-addressed-release-URL pointer (`/icefall/releases/<commit-12>/`); 'How to share a run' (4-step walk-through using the diagnostic Share UI from Phase 8.A.3); 'How to verify a shared run' (4-step walk-through using the Verify Pasted Log section from Phase 8.A.2b); 'Daily-seed convention' (?seed=YYYY-MM-DD format per docs/PHASES.md:585); 'Local development' (preserved + extended with `npm run gen-atlas`); 'Deploy structure' (canonical URLs for latest/ + releases/<commit>/ + releases/index.json + the auto-redirect routing semantics); 'Architecture' (8-layer overview mirroring docs/ARCHITECTURE.md); 'Performance' (41 KB gzipped JS / 1610-byte atlas / cross-OS atlas-equality CI matrix); 'Browser support'; 'Accessibility' (keyboard-only, prefers-reduced-motion, focus rings); 'Determinism guards' (cross-runtime golden chain table — 7 entries + 4 preset-seeds); 'License' (placeholder note — deferred to Phase 9.A.5 because license choice requires explicit maintainer authorization not provided in this iteration).

accessibility_css_gate_added.evidence: style.css gains 2 new accessibility blocks at the tail (lines 316-348). (a) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override applied to *, *::before, *::after; caps animation-duration: 0.001ms !important + animation-iteration-count: 1 !important + transition-duration: 0.001ms !important + scroll-behavior: auto !important. Per WCAG 2.3 SC + docs/PHASES.md:586 acceptance criterion. The override is preventive — the current page has no animations on the deterministic state-hash chain, but future Phase 9 polish iterations may add UI animations and this gate ensures they honor the user's system preference. (b) :focus-visible outline using the --accent neon-purple color (#c792ea) for high-contrast keyboard-focus indication. The default browser outline is preserved (the rule reinforces it with a specific color); this matters most for the canvas (tabindex=0 from Phase 5.A.2) and the diagnostic page's <input>/<button> elements.

phase_9_split_recorded.evidence: Phase 9 is the FINAL phase per docs/PHASES.md:571-593 with 8 deliverables across documentation / UI / art tuning / audio / accessibility. Several deliverables require external input or human aesthetic judgment that an unattended iteration cannot provide. The Phase 9 → 9.A.* sub-phase split is recorded in this approval's next_phase field: 9.A.1 (this approval — README polish + a11y CSS), 9.A.2 (title screen UI), 9.A.3 (theme registry refactor — all UI text routed through src/ui/theme/), 9.A.4 (CRT/scanline post-processing shader, toggleable), 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation — REQUIRES maintainer license-choice authorization; will surface as phase-blocked if not provided), 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md — depends on 9.A.5), 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram), 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit), 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2). Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe playtesting items remain blocked on external input and may surface as phase-blocked in subsequent iterations; they are NOT part of the Phase 9 acceptance criteria so the v1 release can ship without them (a defensible v1.0 scope; audio + extras can be a v1.1 polish update).

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1154 passed across 81 test files (unchanged from Phase 8.B). npm run build: 139.20 KB raw / 41.30 KB gzipped JS (unchanged); 4.26 KB raw / 1.36 KB gzipped CSS (was 3.99 / 1.24 in 8.B = +0.27 KB raw / +0.12 KB gzipped from the 2 new accessibility CSS blocks). Well under the 110 KB CI gate. Cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) preserved unchanged.

no_regression_to_prior_phases.evidence: Phase 9.A.1 touches only README.md + style.css. The README has no executable code; the CSS additions are scoped to media-query gating + a single :focus-visible selector — neither affects the deterministic core's byte output, the renderer's pixel output, or the canvas's keyboard-input handling. The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests pass) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests pass) — preserved unchanged. tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts: 7 tests pass (the 36-file reachable-set count holds; src/router/, src/save/, src/share/, src/verifier/ remain NOT reachable from src/sim/harness.ts — Phase 8 addendum B8 anti-cycle defense intact).

follow_ups_carried_forward: S2 from Phase 7.A.2b (drift sweep candidate, deferred yet again) — tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts:47-48 and tests/sim/turn.test.ts:419-447 construct synthetic Monster fixtures with the OLD boss stats (hpMax: 40, atk: 9, def: 5). Phase 9.A.1 does not touch these test files; future drift sweep is the next opportunity.

All Phase 8 follow-ups (S1-S5 from 8.A.2b, N1-N3 + S1-S5 + O1-O3 from 8.A.3, etc.) carry forward to Phase 9. The Phase 8 polish targets (split determinism/no-float-arithmetic into two rules; pin computeDefinePayload contract; share-button input pre-validation; applyRouting JSDOM-friendly extraction for unit tests; replay-section UX wording; defensive comments in the router; Phase 9 banner UI for auto-redirect errors) are all candidates for Phase 9.A.7 (architecture polish) or Phase 9.A.4 (CRT shader UX touch-up).

Phase 9 LICENSE creation (9.A.5) is BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. The README.md notes 'A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights reserved by the maintainer.' If unattended iteration reaches 9.A.5 without an explicit license preference in the conversation history or repo, it should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with `blocking_question: 'Which license should the project ship under (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other)?'` so the maintainer can resolve the choice externally.

Phase 9 audio (CC-0/CC-BY SFX + synthwave tracks per docs/PHASES.md:581) is BLOCKED on external asset sourcing or commission. Audio is NOT in the Phase 9 acceptance criteria so the v1 release can ship without it (a defensible v1.0 scope); audio surfaces as a v1.1 polish update.

Phase 9 itch.io page + custom domain (per docs/PHASES.md:584 — explicitly marked 'Optional') are BLOCKED on maintainer account ownership / DNS access. Both are explicitly optional; the GH-Pages deploy at https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ is the canonical v1 distribution channel.

Phase 9 atlas-recipe tuning pass (per docs/PHASES.md:579) requires extensive playtesting against the live deployed preview + final atlas-seed choice for v1. The current default seed (ATLAS_SEED_DEFAULT in src/atlas/params.ts) is the candidate; bumping it post-release is a rulesetVersion bump (per acceptance criterion 4). An unattended iteration cannot make the aesthetic judgment; this surfaces as phase-blocked at the relevant sub-phase if/when the maintainer wants to defer the choice.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9 is the FIRST phase in the project where the docs/PHASES.md scope explicitly includes both sandbox-resolvable items (UI implementation, theme registry, accessibility, README polish) AND external-input items (audio assets, itch.io, license, atlas tuning). The unattended-mode operating rules (`If genuine external input is required, write artifacts/phase-blocked.json and stop`) suggest splitting Phase 9 across multiple iterations: do the sandbox-resolvable chunks one at a time, surface each external-blocked item as a phase-blocked artifact when reached. Phase 9.A.1 (this approval) opens the split.

The README rewrite is the highest-leverage Phase 9.A.1 deliverable — the prior README claimed 'Phase 1: in progress' which would mislead any first-time visitor about the project's current state. After this iteration, a stranger landing on https://github.com/porkchop/icefall sees an accurate description of the deterministic-shareable-roguelike system that Phase 8 actually shipped. The README also documents the daily-seed convention (per docs/PHASES.md:585 deliverable) and the share/verify workflows (which are user-facing surfaces that Phase 8 added but didn't document).

The :focus-visible outline + prefers-reduced-motion gate are preventive accessibility additions — the page currently has no animations to throttle and the canvas is the primary keyboard-focus target. Future Phase 9 polish (CRT shader animation, title-screen transitions, share-button feedback animations) will benefit from the gate already being in place. WCAG 2.3 SC compliance is addressed.

Phase 9.A.2 (title screen UI) is the next sandbox-resolvable chunk. Per Phase 9 acceptance criterion 1 (`A first-time visitor can land on the GitHub Pages URL, click 'New Run,' and play to floor 1 without reading docs`), the title screen is load-bearing for v1. The current page goes straight into the playable game on a hardcoded seed; a real title screen needs: a seed input field + 'New Run' button + 'Random Seed' button (calls fingerprintFull on a date-derived seed) + 'Paste Fingerprint' button (calls parseShareUrl + decideRouting). The UI is implementable in src/ui/title-screen.ts (a peer of src/ui/win-screen.ts from Phase 7) + a small main.ts orchestration change (render the title screen before the game canvas; transition on 'New Run' click).
The next iteration should pick up phase-9.A.2 (title screen) as the next sandbox-resolvable chunk, with an explicit phase-blocked artifact if it reaches 9.A.5 (LICENSE) without authorization.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.2 — title-screen UI per docs/PHASES.md:582 deliverable + acceptance criterion 1. Lands src/ui/title-screen.ts as a peer of src/ui/win-screen.ts from Phase 7; renders before the playable game canvas; provides a seed input field + 'New Run' button (boots a fresh run with the current build's commitHash + rulesetVersion + the seed + empty mods) + 'Random Seed' button (uses today's date in YYYY-MM-DD form per the daily-seed convention from 9.A.1) + 'Paste Fingerprint' button (opens a small modal / textarea that accepts a `?run=&seed=` URL or a 22-char fingerprint; routes through parseShareUrl + decideRouting). On click of 'New Run', the title screen hides and the game canvas + HUD + inventory + equipment panels render. The keyboard-only navigation contract is preserved (focus rings from 9.A.1 visible; Enter / Space activate buttons; Tab cycles inputs). Includes tests for the title-screen DOM structure + e2e tests for the click → boot transition. After 9.A.2: 9.A.3 (theme registry refactor — route every UI text constant through src/ui/theme/strings.ts so a future theme mod can replace it; per acceptance criterion 3); 9.A.4 (CRT/scanline post-processing shader, toggleable, per docs/PHASES.md:580); 9.A.5 (LICENSE — BLOCKED on maintainer authorization; surfaces as phase-blocked.json if reached without explicit choice); 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md — depends on 9.A.5); 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram per docs/PHASES.md:583); 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit per docs/PHASES.md:586); 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2). Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input and are NOT in Phase 9 acceptance criteria — they are deferred to a v1.1 polish update.
